description: Analyze a script-grounded operation and produce a dry-run execution contract with entrypoints, arguments, dependencies, side effects, mutation_avoided=true, and verifier notes. It does not load, execute, mutate, or parse arbitrary files by itself.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: Given a DOI, use the CrossRef API to fetch metadata and return a BibTeX citation. Handle errors gracefully.

bibtex.description: BibTeX citation string, or error message if conversion fails

postconditions: Output contains either a valid BibTeX entry or an error message
preconditions: Network access to api.crossref.org is available

name: script_dry_run_analyzer_script_skillsbench_04_doi_to_bibtex_scripts

challenges.expected_evidence: A BibTeX string that can be parsed by a standard BibTeX parser.
challenges.failure_signal: Returns an error message, empty string, or invalid format.
rubric: Produces a valid BibTeX string starting with '@'
Contains correct title, author, and year fields
Uses the provided DOI in the BibTeX key or entry
challenges.task: Convert a valid DOI to its corresponding BibTeX citation string.

challenges.expected_evidence: A string that explains the failure, not a BibTeX entry.
challenges.failure_signal: Returns a valid BibTeX for an invalid DOI, or crashes.
rubric: Returns a clear error message indicating the DOI is invalid or not found
Does not crash or raise an unhandled exception
Returns a non-BibTeX string (e.g., error description)
challenges.task: Handle an invalid or non-existent DOI gracefully.

challenges.expected_evidence: A string indicating network failure after a reasonable timeout.
challenges.failure_signal: Crashes with an unhandled exception or hangs.
rubric: Returns a user-friendly error message about network failure
challenges.task: Handle network timeout or API unavailability.

constraints: Requires internet access to reach the CrossRef API.
The DOI must be valid and resolvable by CrossRef.
API rate limits may apply; excessive requests can be blocked.
The script does not handle all edge cases (e.g., missing fields).
facts: The script uses the CrossRef API to fetch metadata for a given DOI.
A requests session is initialized with a custom user-agent header.
The conversion returns a BibTeX-formatted string.
The script is intended as a quick utility for citation management.
procedures: Initialize a requests.Session with a user-agent header.
Send a GET request to https://api.crossref.org/works/{doi}.
Parse the JSON response to extract metadata (e.g., title, authors, year).
Format the extracted data into a BibTeX entry string.
context_pack.summary: A Python script that converts a DOI (Digital Object Identifier) to a BibTeX citation using the CrossRef API. It initializes a requests session with a user-agent header and provides a class DOIConverter for conversion.
